title: MongoDB Authentication & RBAC - Complete Guide (Beginner to Advanced)
description: Comprehensive guide on user authentication, roles, permissions, and role-based access control

authentication_rbac_overview.definition: RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) is a security model that grants permissions to roles, then assigns roles to users
purpose: Control what data users can access
Control what operations users can perform
two_components: Authentication: Who are you? (username/password, X.509, LDAP)
Authorization: What can you do? (roles and permissions)

definitions.definition: Determining what user can do (what can you do?)
definitions.example: User role has read access to students collection
dbAdmin role has full database access

definitions.definition: Permission to perform action on resource

section_2_beginner_user_creation.title: BEGINNER: Creating Users
section_2_beginner_user_creation.definition: Users must be created with credentials and assigned roles

beginner_examples.title: Create admin user
Create application user
beginner_examples.access: Read and write to all collections

beginner_examples.title: Create read-only user

important_notes: Users created in 'admin' database apply globally
Users created in specific database are limited to that database
Passwords must be strong (minimum requirements vary)

section_3_beginner_builtin_roles.title: BEGINNER: Built-in Roles
section_3_beginner_builtin_roles.definition: MongoDB provides predefined roles for common scenarios

database_roles.description: Read and write to any collection

database_roles.operations: All database management, no data modification

admin_roles.operations: Create users, grant roles, change passwords

beginner_examples.title: Assign read role
beginner_examples.setup: User can only read, no modifications

beginner_examples.title: Assign readWrite role
beginner_examples.setup: User can read and modify data

section_4_intermediate_custom_roles.title: INTERMEDIATE: Custom Roles
section_4_intermediate_custom_roles.definition: Create custom roles with specific permissions for fine-grained access control

intermediate_examples.title: Teacher role (read + update grades)
Advisor role (read student data)
Audit role (read-only on all)
intermediate_examples.permissions: Read from all collections in database

section_5_intermediate_role_inheritance.title: INTERMEDIATE: Role Inheritance
section_5_intermediate_role_inheritance.definition: Roles can inherit permissions from other roles

intermediate_examples.title: Department head (inherits teacher)
intermediate_examples.inheritance: Inherits all teacher permissions plus admin operations
intermediate_examples.use_case: Department manager with additional admin powers

intermediate_examples.title: Support staff (inherits advisor)
intermediate_examples.inheritance: Inherits advisor read + can update students
intermediate_examples.use_case: Support team with limited update access

section_7_advanced_granular_permissions.title: ADVANCED: Granular Permissions
section_7_advanced_granular_permissions.definition: Fine-grained control at collection or field level

advanced_examples.title: Collection-specific role
advanced_examples.effect: User can modify only grades, read only students

advanced_examples.title: Index management role
advanced_examples.effect: User can only manage indexes, not data

advanced_examples.title: Backup operator role
advanced_examples.effect: User can only read statistics for backups

advanced_examples.title: Least privilege principle
advanced_examples.example: Teacher role: only grades update, not student records

advanced_examples.title: Role hierarchy
advanced_examples.pattern: Base roles → Manager roles → Admin roles

advanced_examples.title: Service accounts
advanced_examples.example: app_user with readWrite on specific collections

advanced_examples.title: Temporary access
advanced_examples.pattern: Create temporary users with time limit
advanced_examples.note: Delete when access no longer needed

intermediate.setup: Custom role: read students, update grades

advanced.setup: Complex role hierarchy with read-only access everywhere
Per-tenant roles limiting to tenant database

practice_1_principle_of_least_privilege.good: Teacher role: update grades only, not students

practice_2_use_custom_roles.rule: Create custom roles instead of using built-in

practice_3_regular_audits.check: Remove inactive users, verify appropriate access

practice_5_service_accounts.good: app_user for application, separate from admin

mistake_1_root_for_everything.solution: Create custom roles for each function

mistake_4_excessive_privileges.mistake: Give dbOwner to data entry staff

summary.core_concept: RBAC: Assign permissions to roles, assign roles to users
two_parts: Authorization: Grant permissions (what can you do)
summary.best_practice: Least privilege - grant minimum necessary access
summary.progression: Beginner: Built-in roles → Intermediate: Custom roles → Advanced: Granular control and hierarchies
